title: climate-data-deletion-hurricane-season — 2026-05-15
narrative: The Trump administration has deleted or taken offline massive amounts of federal climate data — including Department of Defense satellite weather data feeds used by hurricane forecasters. NOAA and other agencies have been directed to remove climate references from agency websites. The official position: the climate crisis is exaggerated or fabricated, and federal resources shouldn't prioritize what officials call "climate advocacy." The timing is notable: the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season begins June 1. Hurricane forecasters at NOAA and academic institutions rely on decades of sea surface temperature data, atmospheric moisture readings, and storm tracking information — much of it collected by federal satellites — to make the seasonal and intraseasonal predictions that coastal communities use to prepare. If that data disappears, the forecasts get worse. People die.
telemetry: AP: hurricane forecasters specifically warned that cutting DoD satellite data feeds will "severely hamper" forecasts — this warning was issued before the 2026 hurricane season

Science and Environmental Journalism (SEJ): federal climate and weather disaster data is being systematically deleted or hidden from public access while billion-dollar weather disasters continue to occur
Carbon Brief: Trump's official climate report contains 100+ false or misleading claims checked and documented by climate scientists
The Guardian (May 7): the administration is actively deleting raw datasets — not merely changing narrative language on websites, but removing the underlying data that scientists use for modeling

EU Copernicus Climate Change Service and private weather firms are now the primary global trackers of US climate data — the US government is ceding scientific monitoring authority to foreign and independent entities
NOAA's National Hurricane Center issues forecasts that protect roughly 30 million people in coastal US states; degraded data inputs directly reduce forecast accuracy
analysis: The internal logic is stark and damning. If climate change were real and dangerous — as the Trump administration officially believes it is not — then deleting the data infrastructure that tracks it would be a catastrophic mistake. But the administration doesn't believe climate change is real. They believe it's exaggerated or fabricated. So from their perspective, deleting climate data is simply cleaning up a wasteful federal program. The problem is that the data being deleted isn't just climate data. It's weather data. It's the satellite feeds that track storm systems, measure ocean temperatures, and feed the models that predict where hurricanes go and how strong they get. These are not contested climate theories — they are the raw inputs to operational weather forecasting that saves lives. When you cut DoD satellite data feeds to hurricane models, you don't get a slightly worse forecast. You get a significantly degraded forecast with months of lead time gone before you realize the degradation. The EU's Copernicus service and private weather firms are now in the position of being the world's primary source of US climate monitoring data. This is not a small irony: an administration that claims climate science is a foreign hoax is forcing US scientific monitoring capacity to be replaced by European scientific monitoring capacity.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The administration is deleting the fire hoses while the house burns. The hurricane season starting June 1 is not theoretical — it's a deadline. Forecasters are already warning that degraded data will make the 2026 hurricane season more dangerous for coastal residents who rely on NOAA warnings. The Carbon Brief's documentation of 100+ false claims in the official climate report tells you everything about the administration's relationship to data: they don't engage with it. They select it. And now they're deleting the parts they don't like while the parts they rely on for hurricane tracking go with them.
